compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

`src/rebar/_store/push.py` async mode spawns a detached child (`start_new_session=True`,
`close_fds=True`, streams to `DEVNULL`, reconstructed `PYTHONPATH`) whose whole purpose
is to finish the push AFTER the calling process exits. The current E2E
(`tests/interfaces/store/test_push_policy_e2e.py`) writes in-process and polls origin
while the parent stays alive — proving "lands in background," not "survives parent
exit." Spawn-failure logging and generic `PUSH_PENDING` surfacing are already covered
elsewhere, so only the parent-exit survival contract is untested.

## What

- Add a forced-order test proving the detached push completes after the spawning CLI
  process has exited.
- Order the interaction with a controlled `git` wrapper on `PATH` that blocks at
  `git push` behind a release file, so the parent provably exits before the child's
  push completes.

## Scope

- `tests/interfaces/store/` (new parent-exit survival test)
- A test fixture `git` shim (passes non-push commands through; blocks on push).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A `git` shim on `PATH` passes non-push commands through and, on `git push`, writes
      an "entered push" marker and blocks on a release file/pipe.
- [ ] Launch `rebar create` with async push in a subprocess; wait for the "entered push"
      marker; assert the CLI parent has EXITED while the push child is still blocked.
- [ ] Release the push; poll the bare origin and assert the exact created event/commit
      arrives on the configured remote branch.
- [ ] The test does not re-cover spawn-failure logging or `PUSH_PENDING` (already tested).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
compiled_state.title: Add async-push-survives-parent-exit test
